Japanese: 証人！　‥‥まったく、 お話になりません！
English: Witness! Your testimony is a joke!

Japanese: え‥‥え‥‥？ わ、わたしが、何か‥‥
English: Huh... Wh-What...? B-But I... I just...

Japanese: コラ、弁護人！　シゲキしては イケナイと言ったでしょうが！
English: Ms. Fey! I thought I warned you not to make the witness cry!

Japanese: １回の証言で、２つのムジュン‥‥
English: One short testimony and two bad contradictions...

Japanese: これはもう、イイワケの しようがありません！
English: There's no possible excuse!

Japanese: ムジュンが‥‥ “２つ”ですって！
English: You say there were... two contradictions!?

Japanese: ハナシはカンタンです。 上面図を見ていただきましょう。
English: It's simple. Just take a look at the diagram of the area.

Japanese: 証言によれば、２人は 吊り橋のまん中にいました。
English: According to her testimony, the two of them were in the middle of the bridge.

Japanese: もし、ここで‥‥
English: But if they were, and the victim had turned around and tried to run...

Japanese: 被害者が“犯人に背中を向けて” 逃げようとしたら‥‥？
English: Well, then...

Japanese: い‥‥行き止まり‥‥
English: Sh-She would've hit a dead-end...!

Japanese: １０メートルどころか、 ５メートルも逃げられません！
English: You said 10 yards, but she couldn't have ran even 5!

Japanese: おぼろ橋は、 コワれていたのですから！
English: Because Dusky Bridge is collapsed on that side!

Japanese: きゃああああッ！
English: Waaaaaah!

Japanese: ここ、これはいったい、 どういうことなのかッ！
English: Wh-What does all this mean!?

Japanese: 状況は、きわめてシンプルです。
English: It's very simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: かわいらしいおじょうさんが、 かわいらしいウソをついた。
English: This charming, little witness told a charming, little lie.

Japanese: ‥‥それだけのことです。
English: That's all there is to it.
Nnnn...ggg...urk...

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか！　この、 かわいらしいおじょうさんが‥‥
English: Th-This beautiful, young lady has been l-lying to the court...?

Japanese: ‥‥お待ちいただこう。
English: Just a moment, Your Honor.

Japanese: （み、御剣検事‥‥！）
English: (M-Mr. Edgeworth...!)

Japanese: ただ今の騒ぎ‥‥私のほうから、 おワビを申し上げよう。
English: Your Honor... Allow me to personally apologize for the confusion.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: この上面図には、１つ‥‥ 大きな問題があったのだ。
English: There's one major mistake in this diagram.

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What did you say!?

Japanese: いったい、それは！
English: What are you referring to?

Japanese: 《事件発生後に作成した》 という点だ。
English: It's all because this diagram was made after the incident occurred.

Japanese: なにぶん、山奥の古ぼけた吊り橋。 ‥‥見取り図がなかったのだ。
English: It's a very old bridge... We couldn't find any official blueprints of it.

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥？
English: S-So you're saying...?

Japanese: 事件が発生したとき、このように 橋がコワれていたか‥‥？
English: I'm saying that even though this bridge is currently in disrepair...

Japanese: 今となっては、 何者にも証言できないのだ。
English: ...there's no evidence that can prove that the bridge was broken during the incident.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥ッ！
English: Th-That's ridiculous...!

Japanese: ジッサイ、この写真では‥‥ 橋の状況は、判別がつかない。
English: You can't actually tell the condition of the bridge from this photo...

Japanese: 不完全な証拠品で、審理を コンランさせてしまったようだ‥‥
English: I apologize to the court for not being more clear when I presented the evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥ぬ。 ぬうううううううううむ‥‥ッ！
English: Hmm. Hmmmm...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　やるじゃねえか。
English: Ha...! That guy is good.
Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: コイツは、仕組まれてたんだ。
English: He planned it from the beginning.

Japanese: あのボウヤ‥‥上面図に、 最初から保険をかけていやがった。
English: He's a genius alright... That diagram of the bridge was his insurance policy.

Japanese: （そんな‥‥ヒキョウな！）
English: (What!? That coward!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人の 勇み足だったようですな。
English: Well, Ms. Fey... It seems you've once again made a reckless accusation.

Japanese: すみません‥‥。わたしが しっかりしてないばかりに‥‥
English: I-I'm so sorry! I should have been more careful myself...!

Japanese: いやいやいやいやいやいやいや。 あなたのせいではありません！
English: No, no, no, no! It wasn't YOUR fault at all!

Japanese: それでは‥‥審理を 再開していただこう。
English: Now then... Shall we go on with the trial?

Japanese: 証人の目撃したこと‥‥ そろそろ、決着をつけたい。
English: I'd like to establish, once and for all, what it was that the witness actually saw.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: それでは、おじょうさん。
English: Alright, young lady...

Japanese: もう一度だけ、証言を おねがいできますかな？
English: May I ask you to please proceed with your testimony?

Japanese: わたし‥‥へこたれませんわ！
English: But I... It's so hard to go on...!

Japanese: ここで応援しておりますぞ！
English: We're all on your side, Ms. Foster!

Japanese: シンパイはいらない。 見たコトだけを、証言してほしい。
English: There's no need to worry. Just tell us what you saw.

Japanese: はいっ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: ～目撃‥‥犯行から逃走まで～
English: -- Running From the Crime --

Japanese: お背中を刺された被害者を、 犯人はすばやく抱え上げました。
English: After he stabbed her in the back, he quickly picked her up in his arms.

Japanese: そしてそのまま犯人は、 彼女をお運びになったのです。
English: Then he carried her over to the car.

Japanese: 運び去るほかに、死体をかくす 方法など、ありませんものね。
English: I suppose that was the only way he could make sure the body stayed hidden.

Japanese: 死体を橋の上に残しておくわけには いかなかったのでしょう。
English: He couldn't just leave the body on top of the bridge.

Japanese: わたし‥‥今度こそ、見たコトしか お話ししていませんわ。
English: Oh, I'm sorry... I'm only supposed to talk about what I saw.

Japanese: ぬぬううううう‥‥。 そんな光景を目撃したのですか。
English: Hmm... Witnessing such violence must have been difficult.

Japanese: ええ。わたし、もうショックで‥‥ おムネがドキドキいたしましたの。
English: Yes, sir... I-I'm still shaken up...

Japanese: この証言が通っちまったら‥‥ もう、オシマイだな。
English: If he accepts this testimony as it is, we're finished.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: D-Don't say that...!

Japanese: さて。‥‥帰りに、カフェーに 寄っていこうじゃねえか。
English: Oh well... Maybe I'll stop off at my favorite café on the way home.

Japanese: ‥‥うまいモカを飲ませる 店を知っているぜ。
English: They make a really great mocha latté...

Japanese: 裁判は‥‥まだ、終わらせません！
English: This trial isn't over yet!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ それもいいだろうさ‥‥。
English: Ha...! That's what I like to hear...

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします！
English: Alright, Ms. Fey. Your cross-examination, if you please.

Japanese: （ムジュンは目の前にあるわ。 一気に突きくずすの、千尋！）
English: (The contradiction is staring you right in the face, Mia! Go on the attack!)

Japanese: ～目撃‥‥犯行から逃走まで～
English: -- Running From the Crime --

Japanese: お背中を刺された被害者を、 犯人はすばやく抱え上げました。
English: After he stabbed her in the back, he quickly picked her up in his arms.

Japanese: そしてそのまま犯人は、彼女を お運びになったのです。
English: Then he carried her over to the car.

Japanese: 運び去るほかに、死体をかくす 方法など、ありませんものね。
English: I suppose that was the only way he could make sure the body stayed hidden.

Japanese: 死体を橋の上に残しておくわけには いかなかったのでしょう。
English: He couldn't just leave the body on top of the bridge.

Japanese: わたし‥‥今度こそ、見たコトしか お話ししていませんわ。
English: Oh, I'm sorry... I'm only supposed to talk about what I saw.

Japanese: （気になる点が１つ、あるわ。 でも、それがいったい‥‥）
English: (Something about this testimony is bothering me, but what...?)

Japanese: アンタ‥‥コーヒーに シオを入れたことは？
English: Hey, Kitten... Have you ever put salt in your coffee?

Japanese: あ、あるワケないでしょう！
English: No... Why would I!?

Japanese: なぜだ？
English: Why not?
Huh...?

Japanese: サトウなんか入れるより、 よっぽどイケるかもしれねえぜ？
English: It may actually go better with coffee than sugar, right?

Japanese: いいか。迷うぐらいなら、 たっぷりシオを入れて飲みほせ。
English: Listen. My point is if you're not sure, you might as well add a ton of salt to it.

Japanese: せっかくの証拠が‥‥ サビついちゃうぜ。
English: It might... bring out the rust in something. Like a piece of evidence.

Japanese: （そうよ、千尋‥‥。 とにかく、つきつけてみるの！）
English: (He's right, Mia... Go present something. You've got nothing to lose!)

Japanese: ちなみに、コーヒーにシオは、 やっぱり合わねえが‥‥な。
English: By the way, I wouldn't put salt in my coffee. The two don't go well, after all.

Japanese: 犯人は、盗んだクルマのトランクを コジ開けて、死体をかくしました。
English: The killer broke into the trunk of the stolen car and hid the body in there.

Japanese: 被害者は、橋の上に 倒れなかった、ということですか？
English: Are you saying that the victim didn't fall down on the bridge?

Japanese: え‥‥あ。そ、そうですね。 もしかしたら、お倒れになったかも
English: Err... Umm... Actually, maybe she did fall.

Japanese: もちろん、倒れなかったのだ。
English: Of course she didn't fall down on the bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥そうでなければ、 この写真の説明がつかない。
English: If she had fallen down, this photo wouldn't make any sense.

Japanese: もし、被害者が倒れていれば‥‥ コートが汚れているはずだ。
English: If that was the case, her coat would've been all muddy.

Japanese: 私は、証人に聞いているのです！
English: If you don't mind, I was asking the witness!

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。 これはシツレイ。
English: *sigh* No need to be so rude.

Japanese: それで‥‥いかがでしたかな？ おじょうさん。
English: Well, young lady?

Japanese: モチロン、お倒れには なりませんでしたわ。
English: Of course she didn't fall down.

Japanese: その前に、囚人服のかたが 抱きとめましたから。
English: The man in the prison uniform grabbed her before she could.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 一足おそかったようだな‥‥
English: Ha...! We're one step too slow...

Japanese: それで‥‥そのあと、 被告人は、どうしたか‥‥？
English: And then, what did the defendant do after that?

Japanese: あなたは、それを 目撃していたのですか？
English: You personally witnessed that?

Japanese: ええ‥‥そうですわ。
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: そのとき、何か変わったことは ありませんでしたか？
English: Did anything strange happen when he did that?

Japanese: 変わったことかどうか わかりませんけど‥‥
English: Well, I don't know if you'd call it strange or not...

Japanese: 被害者の女のかたの おマフラーが落ちましたわ。
English: ...but that's when the victim's scarf fell off.

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥ この、おマフラーがッ！
English: Hmm... You mean this scarf!?

Japanese: 現場の状況とも一致する。 問題あるまい‥‥
English: Her words match what we found at the scene. I don't see any problem...

Japanese: 犯人は、たった１人で 死体を運んだんですか？
English: You mean the defendant carried the body all by himself?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: 被告人の体格ならば、 ムズカシイことではあるまい。
English: Considering the size of the defendant, I don't think it would be difficult.

Japanese: でも‥‥現場は、細く コワれかけた吊り橋です！
English: Yes, but... Let's remember they were on a narrow bridge that was ready to collapse!

Japanese: そんな場所で、死体を運ぶなんて ‥‥可能だったのでしょうか。
English: Is it even possible for him to have carried a dead body on a bridge like that...?

Japanese: しかし、ジッサイ、死体は 運び去られていたのだ。
English: Well, the fact of the matter is that he DID!

Japanese: ‥‥そのギロンには、 イミがない！
English: That kind of talk is just silly!

Japanese: （何かしら‥‥？ 急に感情的になって‥‥）
English: (Wow... Why did he get so emotional all of a sudden?)

Japanese: 他に方法があったと言うのならば、 あなたが示すべき、でしょうね。
English: Ms. Fey, if you think there's some other possibility, please share it with the rest of us.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜですか？ 山奥の吊り橋の上ですよ？
English: Why do you say that? It's already a broken-down bridge hidden away in the mountains.

Japanese: わざわざ死体を持ち去る 必要など、なかったのでは‥‥？
English: Doing anything more to hide the corpse would be going overboard, wouldn't it?

Japanese: 吾童山は、ハイキングコースが 有名ですの。
English: Yes, but that mountain is famous among hikers.

Japanese: 意外に、人が集まりますのよ。
English: A surprising number of people go up there.

Japanese: でも、今は２月で、事件当日には 雨もふっていたんですよ？
English: But it's February, right? And it was raining that day, correct?

Japanese: 小さなお寺や霊場もありますの。
English: There is also a small temple and a channeling dojo there.

Japanese: 修行者のかたは、サムいところが お好みのようですから‥‥。
English: You know those monks... They just love cold, isolated places...

Japanese: つまり、死体はいつ発見されても おかしくない状況だった‥‥
English: I think the witness is trying to say that the corpse could have been found at any time...

Japanese: ジッサイ、この証人が 目撃していたわけだからな。
English: Besides, the witness is merely reporting what she witnessed with her own eyes.

Japanese: それで‥‥死体を運んだのは、 まちがいなく被告人でしたか？
English: And you're absolutely certain that it was my client who was carrying the body?

Japanese: 犯人は、囚人さんの服を着ていたの ですけど‥‥おカオまでは‥‥
English: Well, he was wearing a prisoner's uniform, but... as for his face...

Japanese: ハッキリ見えたワケでは ないのですね？
English: So you're saying you didn't get a clear look at his face?

Japanese: キョリもあったし、 お雨も降っておりましたから。
English: Well, they were far away and it was raining as well.

Japanese: わたし‥‥見たコトしか 言わないほうがいいと思って‥‥
English: I-I thought I was only supposed to say exactly what I saw...

Japanese: ぬううううううう！ リッパなココロがけです！
English: Excellent! You're a remarkably honest young woman!

Japanese: そのあと、犯人は どうしましたか？
English: What did the man do then?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥クルマに お乗りになって、お逃げに。
English: Well... Naturally, he got in the car and was about to flee.

Japanese: そこでわたし、やっと ワレに返ったんです。
English: That's... That's when I came to my senses.

Japanese: 『おまわりさんに知らせないと！』 そう思って‥‥
English: I said to myself, "You have to call the police!"

Japanese: ‥‥それで、 警察に通報したわけ、だ。
English: And so that's when you called the police?

Japanese: あなたは、それを 目撃したわけですね？
English: You're sure that you saw all that with your own eyes?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ、まちがいありません。
English: Yes. I'm 100% certain.

Japanese: 犯人が死体をかくしたかった‥‥ それは、リカイできます。
English: A killer not wanting his victim to be found... I can understand that.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そのために死体を 運ぶのは、アキラカに不自然です。
English: However! The idea of moving the body for that purpose is clearly odd.

Japanese: 犯人には、もっとラクでカクジツな 方法があったのですから！
English: There was a much easier way to make sure the body wasn't found!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥いったい！
English: W-Well? What is it!?

Japanese: 上面図を見れば、アキラカです。
English: Take another look at the map of the area and you'll see how.

Japanese: 吊り橋の下に、 川が流れています。
English: There's a river right below the bridge.

Japanese: たしか、検事さんによれば‥‥
English: Earlier Mr. Edgeworth pointed out something interesting about the river.

Japanese: 吊り橋の下を流れる《吾童川》は、 その水量と急流で知られている。
English: For those who are not aware, Eagle River is well-known for its powerful current.

Japanese: のみこまれた死体は、ほとんど 発見されることがないという‥‥
English: Most bodies that fall in are never recovered...
Ahh...

Japanese: 現に、５年前の誘拐事件では、 被害者は川に投げこまれている‥‥
English: In the kidnapping case 5 years ago, the victim's body was carried away and never found.

Japanese: これから先、おぼろ橋で１０回 殺人事件が起こるとしたら‥‥
English: If 10 murders were to occur at that same spot above the Eagle River...

Japanese: 吾童川には、１０人の死体が 放りこまれるにちがいありません！
English: ...you can bet your boots that every other killer would have tossed the body in the water!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人の言い方は、 どうかと思いますが‥‥
English: I'm not sure if I care for the way you put that, Ms. Fey!

Japanese: たしかに！　この状況で、わざわざ 死体を運ぶのは‥‥不自然です！
English: But I must admit it does seem odd not to have thrown the body into the river!

Japanese: ううう‥‥‥！
English: Ooooh...!

Japanese: いかがかな？　御剣検事くん！
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ふう‥‥やれやれ。
English: *sigh* How sad.

Japanese: 弁護人も一度、その《吾童川》に のまれてみてはいかがか。
English: Perhaps Ms. Fey would do well to try taking a dip in the river herself.

Japanese: “不自然”を語る前に、大自然と たわむれるコトをおすすめしよう。
English: After all, you claim to be such an expert in the ways of nature...

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: 死体をトランクにしまうのが、 たとえ“不自然”だろうと‥‥、
English: My point is that no matter how odd you may find the killer's method of body-disposal...

Japanese: ジッサイに、 犯人はそうしているのだ。
English: ...the fact is that this is what the killer did.

Japanese: その理由がムジュンしていても‥‥ この証人には関係ないこと。
English: None of your arguments have anything to do with what the witness saw.

Japanese: ぬ‥‥ぬううううう‥‥ た、たしかに！
English: Hmm... Quite true...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、弁護人の指摘は 決定的とは言えないようです。
English: Ms. Fey, it seems that your assertion is without merit after all.

Japanese: しかし！　証人が目撃したという 犯人の行動は、あまりに不自然‥‥
English: But what the witness claims to have seen is totally ridiculous...

Japanese: それでも、たしかに死体は トランクから発見されたのだ。
English: Surely you can't deny that the body was found in the trunk of the car.

Japanese: その点にムジュンはありません。
English: That's certainly consistent with what the witness has told us.
Ugh...

Japanese: それでは、証人。 証言をつづけていただこう。
English: Please, witness, go on with your testimony.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: I-I'll try...

Japanese: あなたはただ、“見たことだけ”を 証言すればいいのだ。
English: All you have to do is tell us only what you saw.

Japanese: さもないと、イジワルなお姉さんに いじめられてしまうからな。
English: Otherwise the mean lady might yell at you again.

Japanese: （ダレのことよ！）
English: (Who is he talking about!?)

Japanese: わかりましたわ！　わたし‥‥ がんばります！
English: Alright! I-I'll do my best!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥証人。 ついに、やってしまいましたね。
English: Well, Ms. Foster... It looks like you've done it this time.

Japanese: ‥‥‥？　あ、あの‥‥ 何を、ですの？
English: D-Done what?

Japanese: 決定的なミス、です。
English: Made a crucial mistake.
A c-crucial mistake? Like what, Ms. Fey!?

Japanese: 《盗んだクルマのトランクを コジ開けて、死体をかくした》
English: "The killer broke into the trunk of the stolen car and hid the body in there."

Japanese: あなたは、それを“見た” わけですね？　‥‥その目で！
English: You're saying you saw that, right? With your very own eyes?

Japanese: そ、それが何か‥‥？
English: Y-Yes... And...?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。 ‥‥上面図を見てください。
English: It's simple, Ms. Foster. Take a look at the diagram.

Japanese: 事件当時、あなたが写真を撮ったと 主張している地点は、ここです。
English: The place you claim to have taken the photo from that day is here.

Japanese: いかがかしら？ クルマを見ようとしても‥‥
English: Do you see what I mean? Even if you tried to see the car...

Japanese: この岩場が、 どうしてもジャマになる。
English: ...this outcropping of rock is directly in the way.

Japanese: あ‥‥っ！
そうなんです、証人‥‥。
English: That's right, Ms. Foster.

Japanese: あなたのいた地点から、 犯人のクルマが見えるはずがない！
English: From where you were standing, you could not have possibly seen the killer's car.
Aaaaah...!

Japanese: たしかに、上面図には 岩場が表示されている！
English: I admit that the diagram shows a large outcropping of rock.

Japanese: しかし‥‥クルマが見えないほどの 高さかどうかはわからないッ！
English: However... It isn't so tall that it would stop her from seeing the car!

Japanese: そ、そうです！
English: Th-That's right!

Japanese: その岩場は、たいした 高さじゃなくて‥‥
English: It-It's not high at all...

Japanese: わ‥‥わたしには、 あのかたのクルマが見えましたの！
English: I-I was able to see his car just fine!

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥ そうは行きません。
English: I'm so sorry... But that just doesn't wash.

Japanese: 証人は、この法廷に 証拠品を提出してくれましたね？
English: I believe it was the witness who presented this as evidence to the court, yes?

Japanese: 上面図の、このポイントから 撮影した、写真です。
English: This is the location that the photo was taken from.

Japanese: その写真が‥‥ 真実を物語っています！
English: Your own photo tells the whole story!

Japanese: 左はしに‥‥ しっかり写っていますね。
English: You can clearly see the left side of the bridge...

Japanese: 問題の“岩場”‥‥というより、 これはもう、“ガケ”です。
English: But the outcropping that is being referred to is really more like a cliff.

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: あなたの視界は、切り立った ガケにさえぎられていた！
English: Your view should have been completely cut off by this cliff!

Japanese: これでも、犯人のクルマが 見えたと言うつもりですか！
English: But still you claim to have been able to see the killer's car!

Japanese: きゃああああああッ！
English: Noooooo!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ こ‥‥これは、いったい‥‥
English: Or-Order! Order in the court! What is the meaning of all this ballyhoo...!?

Japanese: 裁判長‥‥ 結論を急ぐ必要はない。
English: Your Honor... Don't jump to any hasty conclusions.

Japanese: 脱獄囚がクルマを盗んで逃走した ことは、ニュースでも報道された。
English: The fact that the escapee fled in a stolen car was reported on the news.

Japanese: この証人は、殺人事件を目撃して、 少なからず動揺していたはず。
English: After witnessing a murder, I'm sure you can appreciate that the witness was very upset.

Japanese: クルマのことを聞いて、それを “見た”と思ってしまったのだ。
English: She must have heard about the stolen car and convinced herself that she saw it.

Japanese: 証言に入る前に、彼女は 何度も注意されていました！
English: But she was repeatedly warned before starting her testimony!

Japanese: “見たことだけを 証言するように”‥‥と！
English: She was told to testify only about what she saw with her own eyes!

Japanese: ぬううううううう‥‥む！
English: Hmmmmm... Oh!

Japanese: あの‥‥オジサマ？
English: Err... Mr. Judge?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですかな？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: わたし‥‥きっと、 思いちがいをしていたのですね‥‥
English: I think... I think I must have remembered things wrong...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！ 今さら、そんなイイワケが‥‥
English: Hey! Wait a minute! You can't just say that...!

Japanese: カンちがいをしない人間など、 この世には存在しないのだよ。
English: Ms. Fey, no one on the face of the planet is perfect.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥‥たしかに！ そもそも、カンちがいこそは‥‥
English: Hmmmm... Yes, indeed... Quite true! You know what they say...

Japanese: 人間だけにユルされた、 高度な精神活動ですからな。
English: "To err is human, to forgive, divine."

Japanese: センサイなココロを持った 少女であれば、ムリもない。
English: I'm inclined to give the benefit of the doubt to our witness here.

Japanese: そ‥‥‥そんなバカな‥‥ッ！
English: What...!? Th-That's not fair...!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 冷や汗は後回しだ、コネコちゃん。
English: Ha...! Save the tears for later, Kitten!

Japanese: か‥‥神乃木さん！
English: M-Mr. Armando!

Japanese: 判決が下るまで、ふり返るな。 今は、前へ進むんだ。
English: Don't look back until the trial is over. Now is the time to go forward.

Japanese: で‥‥でも！ ズルいじゃないですか！
English: B-But...! But that wasn't fair!

Japanese: まあ、落ちつけ。 そして‥‥思い出すんだ。
English: OK, Kitten, you need to relax. Then you need to remember...

Japanese: ‥‥あのコネコちゃんの証言を！
English: The other kitten's testimony!

Japanese: 犯人は、盗んだクルマのトランクを コジ開けて、死体をかくしました。
English: The killer broke into the trunk of the stolen car and hid the body in there.

Japanese: クルマのことはともかく‥‥ どうして知ってるんだ？
English: So tell us... How did you know that?

Japanese: 『トランクをコジ開けた』 なんてコトを‥‥
English: How did you know that he broke into the trunk?

Japanese: あ‥‥っ！
English: Aha...!

Japanese: そのことをキレイに 説明できないかぎり‥‥
English: Until you can explain how you knew that...

Japanese: アンタの疑いは晴れねェぜ！ おじょうさんよォ！
English: ...you're going to have a lot of very suspicious people on this side of the courtroom.

Japanese: い‥‥いかがですかな？
English: W-Well, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥トランクがコジ開けられた のは、たしかですわ。
English: Well, I'm certain that he broke into the trunk.

Japanese: だって‥‥トランクのフタに、 キチンとお残りになってますもの。
English: Because... Because there were marks left on the trunk lid.

Japanese: コジ開けられたときの 《引っかきキズ》が‥‥！
English: I'm certain they were scratch marks from when he broke into it...!

Japanese: な‥‥なんとォ！ 写真を見せてください！
English: W-What...!? L-Let me see that photo!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥カギ穴のまわりに キズがあるようです！
English: It's true... These certainly look like scratch marks around the keyhole!

Japanese: ぬうううううううう‥‥。
English: HMMMM...!

Japanese: ダレが見ても“コジ開けられた”と わかりますな。
English: It's obvious that this trunk has been broken open.

Japanese: いかがかな？　弁護人。 ナットクできると思いますが‥‥？
English: Well, Ms. Fey? Are you satisfied...?

Japanese: （‥‥裁判長は彼女の味方。 シッパイはユルされないわ）
English: (The judge is on her side! I can't make any mistakes here!)

Japanese: （今の発言‥‥ ムジュンはなかったかしら‥‥）
English: (What she just said... Is there a contradiction in there somewhere...?)

Japanese: （なんか‥‥引っかかる 気もするんだけどな‥‥）
English: (Something about it still doesn't feel right, but...)

Japanese: そ、そうですね。 問題ない‥‥と思います。
English: I, err... OK, I guess I'm satisfied... Sort of.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、これでギモンは なくなったようだな‥‥
English: I hope this has cleared up any doubts...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Indeed, I believe it has.

Japanese: おいおい、コネコちゃん‥‥。 ジャレてる場合じゃねえぜ。
English: Hey Kitten, this is no time to play around!
Huh...?

Japanese: こんなでっかいムジュンを 見逃すなんて‥‥どうかしてるぜ。
English: Overlooking such a huge contradiction... What am I going do with you?

Japanese: （ムジュン‥‥！）
English: (Contradiction...!?)

Japanese: （じゃあ、やっぱり“アレ”は 気のせいじゃなかったのね！）
English: (So that wasn't my imagination after all!)

Japanese: それでは、次の
English: Very well, let's move on to the next...

Japanese: お‥‥お待ちください！
English: J-Just a minute!

Japanese: なな、なんですか！
English: What is it now, Ms. Fey!?

Japanese: ただ今の証人の発言‥‥ 重大な問題があります！
English: I've got a big problem with what this witness said!

Japanese: たった５秒で主張を 変えないようにッ！
English: I'd appreciate it if you didn't change your mind every 5 seconds!

Japanese: （うう‥‥イタくないわ、千尋！）
English: (Ugh... Hang in there, Mia!)

Japanese: ‥‥無久井　里子さん。
English: Melissa Foster.

Japanese: さっき以上にマズいことを 口走ってしまったみたいね。
English: It looks like you've finally betrayed yourself.
What...!?

Japanese: あなたは、草原で写真を 撮っていたそうですね。
English: You said you were in a field taking photos of wild flowers.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥トランクの キズのことを知っていた。
English: But even so, you knew about the scratches...

Japanese: いったい、いつ！　このキズを 見ることができたのですかっ！
English: The question is when! When did you get a chance to see those scratches!?

Japanese: （やっと‥‥。やっと、 この事件が見えてきた‥‥）
English: (Finally...! I've finally got her!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　チョイと 待ちくたびれちまったぜ。
English: Ha...! I'm getting pretty tired of waiting over here!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人の考えを 聞いたほうがいいようだな‥‥
English: Then perhaps it would be faster if Ms. Fey explained herself.

Japanese: 裁判長！ コタエは１つしかありません！
English: Your Honor! There's only one possible explanation!

Japanese: 証人は、トランクのキズを 見たことがあった！　なぜなら‥‥
English: The reason the witness had seen the scratches was...

Japanese: ‥‥盗まれたクルマは、 証人のものだったのです！
English: Because the stolen car actually belonged to her.

Japanese: 自分の車だからこそ、 キズのコトを知っていた！
English: That is why she knew about the scratches!

Japanese: きっと証人は、現場付近を たまたま通りかかったのです！
English: Because she just happened to be passing by the area where the car was parked!

Japanese: いかがかな？　御剣検事くん。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥ここまでヒドいコタエを 聞かされると‥‥
English: Frankly, Your Honor, after hearing a pathetic response like that...

Japanese: 弁護人の、ニンゲンとしての 存在価値に関わってくると言える。
English: I have to question whether or not such a pathetic lawyer should even exist...

Japanese: ‥‥考えなおしてもらいましょう。 あなたがなぜ、生まれてきたのか。
English: Did you hear that Ms. Fey? I believe Mr. Edgeworth just called you a canucklehead.

Japanese: （なんでそこまで 言われなきゃならないのよ‥‥）
English: (Yeesh, isn't that overdoing things a little bit...?)

Japanese: （あと少し！　もう１歩、 前に出るの‥‥千尋！）
English: (Come on, Mia! One more time! You can do it!)

Japanese: キズを見るカクジツな チャンスが、１つだけあります。
English: There's only one way that the witness had the chance to see those scratches.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: Y-Yes? What was it?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥
English: Naturally...

Japanese: トランクをコジ開けて、 死体をしまうとき‥‥です。
English: When she opened the trunk... and stuffed the corpse in herself!

Japanese: 被害者の死体をトランクに かくしたのは‥‥
English: The person who really hid the body in the trunk of that car was...

Japanese: 無久井　里子さん！ あなた‥‥なのではないですか？
English: Melissa Foster! It was you that did it, wasn't it!?

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！ わたし、そんなコト‥‥
English: Th-That's ridiculous! I could never...!

Japanese: あの、囚人服のかたです！ だって、あのかたが‥‥
English: It was the man in the prison garb! He-He's the one that...

Japanese: そんなハズは、ないの。
English: I don't think so, Ms. Foster.

Japanese: ‥‥もし、尾並田さんが 死体をしまったのなら‥‥
English: If Mr. Fawles had been the one that put the corpse in the trunk...

Japanese: クルマのキーを使ったはずよ。 コジ開ける必要は、ないわ。
English: ...he would have simply used the car key. There was no need to break it open.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！ 盗難車、だったのでしょう‥‥？
English: B-But...! He stole the car...!

Japanese: “信号待ちしていたカップルから 取りあげた”盗難車よ。
English: He stole it from a young couple that had been waiting at a red light.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、クルマには キーがついていたの。
English: Which means that the key would have still been in the ignition.

Japanese: そ‥‥そう、だったの‥‥
English: Oh...! I-I... I see...

Japanese: キズのコト‥‥教えてくれて ありがとう。無久井　里子さん‥‥
English: Thank you for telling us about the scratches, Ms. Foster.

Japanese: おかげで、立証できたようね。
English: Without that, we never would have uncovered the truth.

Japanese: 死体をトランクに入れたのは、 尾並田　美散ではあり得ない！
English: It couldn't have been Mr. Fawles that put the body in the trunk!

Japanese: ‥‥いやああぁぁぁ‥‥ッ！
English: Nooooo!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなッ！　この証人が 死体をかくした‥‥だと！
English: P-Preposterous! To even suggest that the witness put the body in there!

Japanese: それならば‥‥彼女の撮影した、 写真はどう説明するのだ！
English: If that were true... then how do you explain the photo that she took?

Japanese: 死体を入れることができるのは、 事件が起こったときしかない。
English: The corpse could only have been put in the trunk when the incident occurred.

Japanese: その瞬間‥‥彼女は この写真を撮っていたはずだ！
English: And we already know that at the time, she was taking photographs!

Japanese: （今がチャンス‥‥ 一気に攻めこむの、千尋！）
English: (Now is your chance, Mia! Finish this thing!)

Japanese: それはどうかしら、検事さん。
English: On the contrary, I'm not so certain about that anymore, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: むずかしく考える必要は ありません。
English: There's no need to think too deeply about it.

Japanese: この写真‥‥シャッターを押した のは、彼女とはかぎらないのです！
English: What I'm saying is the shutter for this may not have been pushed by Ms. Foster herself!

Japanese: いかがですかっ、裁判長！
English: Well, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥正直に言って‥‥
English: To be quite honest, Ms. Fey...

Japanese: もう少し、むずかしく 考えてほしかったですな。
English: I wish you would have thought a little more deeply on this matter.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: おいおい‥‥こんなところで モタつかないでくれよ。
English: Hey Kitten... Don't start getting all sloppy on me now.

Japanese: アイツは、あそこにいなかった。 でも、写真は撮影された。
English: The fact is, she wasn't there, but the photo got taken.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どうやって‥‥？ カンタンな話だろ。
English: So how did it happen? This is a fastball right over home plate.

Japanese: （私もそう思ったんだけど‥‥）
English: (That's what I thought, but...)

Japanese: もう一度、考えなおして もらいましょうか。
English: Ms. Fey, take a moment and think it over again.

Japanese: 彼女のカメラ‥‥これはもう、 グーゼンとは思えません！
English: I don't think you can make a mistake about it. Take a look at her camera.

Japanese: タイマー撮影ができて、 三脚までついています。
English: It has a timer built into it! Even a mini-tripod!

Japanese: ぬうううううううう‥‥
English: Hmmmm...!

Japanese: まるで、この写真を撮るために 用意されたように！
English: Almost as if the camera was brought just to take this picture!

Japanese: それでは‥‥ 弁護人は、こう主張するつもりか！
English: What are you trying to say then, Ms. Fey!?

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき‥‥ 彼女は草原にいなかった、と！
English: That when the crime occurred, Ms. Foster wasn't in the field as she claimed!?

Japanese: （写真が自動シャッターで 撮影された以上‥‥）
English: (Well, if she really did use the camera's auto-timer...)

Japanese: （当然、彼女は《別の場所》に いたことになる）
English: (...then the answer is yes, she was somewhere else.)

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。 草原には、いませんでした。
English: Exactly. She was NOT in the field.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護側の考えを 聞く必要があるようです。
English: Hmm... Would the defense please explain further.

Japanese: ‥‥いいか‥‥ ここは、重要なポイントだ。
English: Listen... This is a crucial point.

Japanese: “事件が起こったとき、彼女が どこにいたか‥‥？”
English: "Where was Ms. Foster when the incident occurred...?"

Japanese: それがわかれば、おじょうちゃんの “正体”もハッキリするだろうぜ。
English: In answering that question, we'll also make clear Ms. Foster's true identity.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 示していただきましょう。
English: Well then, please answer this question.

Japanese: 橋の上で事件が起こったとき、 無久井　里子さんがどこにいたか？
English: Where was Melissa Foster when the incident on the bridge occurred?

Japanese: もちろん証人は、ここに いたはずです！　‥‥たぶん。
English: Naturally the witness was right... here! ...I think.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥被告人のクルマが 置かれていた地点‥‥ですか。
English: Hmm... In the spot where the defendant's car was?

Japanese: 彼がクルマに戻って来たら、 死体を入れる機会はありません！
English: Yes! She had to put the body in the trunk before the defendant returned!

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけ、うかがおうか。 弁護士クン‥‥
English: You don't mind if I ask one teenie-weenie question, do you, Ms. Fey...?

Japanese: （“クン”と来たか‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. He's got that condescending tone in his voice...)

Japanese: クルマに死体をしまった、 ということは‥‥
English: If she put the body in the trunk at that time, as you suggest...

Japanese: 被害者・美柳　勇希は、その時点で 殺害されていたことになる。
English: ...that must mean that Valerie Hawthorne was already dead at that point, correct?

Japanese: そ、そうなりますな。
English: Hmm, indeed.

Japanese: ‥‥おぼろ橋の上を 見ていただきたい。
English: Please take a look at the top of Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者は、まだ “生きている”ようだが‥‥？
English: It certainly looks to me like the victim is still alive... Am I mistaken?
Err...

Japanese: 美柳　勇希が死んでいるのなら、 これはいったい、どなたかな？
English: If Valerie Hawthorne was already dead, then... who is this?

Japanese: 霊気の強い吾童山のこと‥‥ 殺された被害者の霊、とでも？
English: The mountain is famous for spirits, so maybe you think it was the ghost of the victim?

Japanese: 霊、だったのかも しれません‥‥か？
English: Well, it COULD have been a spirit... Right?

Japanese: 弁護人！　ヒトに聞かない！
English: Ms. Fey! Don't waste the court's time with this kind of foolishness!

Japanese: と、とにかく！　証人は 別の場所にいたハズなんです！
English: A-Anyway! I still maintain that the witness was in a different place at the time!

Japanese: たしかなのですか？
English: Are you certain?

Japanese: たしかです！　‥‥おそらく。
English: Yes, I am certain! More or less.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥ですね。
English: Oh, come now, Ms. Fey...

Japanese: もちろん証人は、ここに 立っていたんです！
English: Naturally, the witness was standing right here!

Japanese: ぬおおおおおおお‥‥ いかがかな？　御剣検事くん。
English: Hmmmmm... Well, what do you think, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: フッ‥‥。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: ヒトが立っている場所を示す前に するべきコトがあるようだ。
English: Before pointing out where the witness was standing, Ms. Fey should do something herself.

Japanese: すなわち、弁護人！　キミ自身が、 どこに立っているのか‥‥？
English: She should figure out where SHE stands, if you catch my drift!

Japanese: （“キミ”と来たか‥‥）
English: (Oh don't worry, the drift was certainly caught...)

Japanese: 弁護士としての自覚を持って、 これからもがんばるように。
English: Yes, failure is an excellent opportunity for growth. Now try better next time.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ、どうも‥‥。
English: Ah, yes... Thank you...

Japanese: もちろん証人は、 ここにいたのです！
English: Naturally, the witness was right here!

Japanese: そ、そ、そこは‥‥
English: B-But that's...

Japanese: 被害者・美柳　勇希さんが 立っていた場所ではないですか！
English: But that's where the victim, Ms. Hawthorne was standing!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 弁護人！　あなたは、いったい‥‥
English: Order! Order! Order! Ms. Fey! What on earth...!?

Japanese: ‥‥よろしいですか、裁判長。
English: Your Honor, if I may...

Japanese: 被告人は、橋の上で“被害者”と 別れたあと、クルマで逃走した。
English: After parting with the "victim" on the bridge, the defendant fled by car.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、これでは被害者が トランクに入ることは不可能です。
English: But this would mean that there was no time to put the victim in the trunk.

Japanese: つまり、だれかが死体を 入れようとしたら‥‥
English: In other words, if someone put the body in the trunk...

Japanese: 被告人が“被害者”と会うよりも、 《前》しかないのです！
English: ...it could only have been before the defendant met the "victim"!

Japanese: バカなッ！　尾並田は、 ジッサイに被害者と会っている！
English: How asinine! Of course Mr. Fawles met with the victim!

Japanese: この状況で、被害者を殺害して、 死体をトランクに入れられたのは、
English: The only person with the opportunity to have put the victim in the trunk...

Japanese: 尾並田　美散以外に、あり得ない！
English: ...is the same man that killed her, Terry Fawles!

Japanese: まだ、わからないのかしら？ 御剣検事‥‥。
English: You still don't understand, do you, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 目撃写真が撮影されたとき、もう 被害者は、殺害されていた‥‥
English: By the time the witness's photo was taken, the victim was already dead...

Japanese: その写真に写っているのは、 美柳　勇希ではないのです！
English: The person in the photo was NOT Valerie Hawthorne!

Japanese: な‥‥何を、バカなコトをッ！ それならば‥‥
English: What!? I've never heard anything more ridiculous in my entire life!

Japanese: この写真に写っている被害者は いったい、何者なのだ！
English: Then who exactly is the "victim" in this photo!?

Japanese: 決まっているでしょう。 ‥‥そこの証人ですよ。
English: It's obvious, isn't it? It's your own witness.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですとぉぉぉ‥‥
English: Wh-Wh-Whaaaat...!?

Japanese: それしか考えられません。
English: It's the only possible explanation.

Japanese: 尾並田さんが橋の上で会ったのは、 美柳　勇希ではなかった！
English: The woman that Mr. Fawles met on the bridge that day was not Valerie Hawthorne!

Japanese: 無久井　里子さん！ あなただったのです！
English: It was you! Melissa Foster!

Japanese: わ‥‥わたし‥‥！
事件があったとき、吾童山には 小雨がふり、キリも出ていました。
English: Let's remember that it was raining and foggy on the mountain that day.

Japanese: 尾並田さんは、目の前の人物を 美柳　勇希さんだと思ったのです！
English: Mr. Fawles himself believed that the woman in front of him was Valerie Hawthorne!

Japanese: 被告人・尾並田　美散は、 美柳　勇希を、よく知っている！
English: But the defendant knew Valerie Hawthorne very well!

Japanese: なにしろ、自分の死刑判決を 決定づけた証人なのだから！
English: After all, she was the woman whose testimony helped get him convicted!

Japanese: それは、５年も前のハナシです！
English: But since then, my client has spent five hard years in a federal penitentiary!

Japanese: 被告人は、美柳　勇希の カオを忘れていたのです！
English: He couldn't remember exactly what she looked like anymore!

Japanese: ‥‥バカなッ！　しょ、 証拠があるとでも言うのかッ！
English: You are just making this up as you go along! Where's your proof!?

Japanese: （あるわ！　あの証拠品が それを立証している‥‥！）
English: (I've got it all right here! This piece of evidence will blow this case wide open...!)

Japanese: 尾並田さんは、事件当時‥‥ 美柳　勇希のカオを忘れていた！
English: At the time of the incident, Mr. Fawles had forgotten what Valerie Hawthorne looked like!

Japanese: 待ちな！
English: Wait a minute, Kitten.

Japanese: か‥‥神乃木さん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Armando...

Japanese: まずは落ちつけ。もう一度、 じっくり急いで考えるんだ！
English: First of all, relax. Second of all, think the whole thing over again!

Japanese: （そ、そうよ！　じっくり 急いで考えるの、千尋！）
English: (H-He's right, Mia! You need to calm down and think it over!)

Japanese: 尾並田さんは、 被害者のカオを忘れていました。
English: Mr. Fawles had forgotten the victim's face.

Japanese: だから‥‥目印が必要だった。 このマフラーが！
English: That's why he needed some piece of identification. Namely, this muddy scarf!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
マフラーは、尾並田さんの 指示で用意されたものでした。
English: It was Mr. Fawles who requested that she wear this scarf to identify herself.

Japanese: 被害者が残したメモが それを立証しています！
English: That's already been proven by the note the victim left!

Japanese: つまり！　指示どおりの マフラーをしていれば‥‥
English: In other words, as long as you were wearing a scarf like he asked...

Japanese: たやすく《美柳　勇希》に なりすますことができたのです！
English: ...anyone could have pretended to be Valerie Hawthorne!

Japanese: さあ！　いかがかしら！ 無久井　里子さん！
English: Well? What do you have to say to that, Melissa Foster!?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ぅぅぅ‥‥ッ！
English: Nnng...Gggh...Unnn...!

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああッ！
English: N-N-N-N-N-Nnnnoooooooo!!!

Japanese: それで‥‥あの、 無久井　里子さんは‥‥？
English: Err... Ahh... Where's Ms. Foster...?

Japanese: 控え室にやすませてある。
English: She's collecting herself in the Lobby.

Japanese: ぬううううううううう‥‥む。
English: Hmmmmm...

Japanese: （無久井　里子のやったことは ハッキリしている‥‥）
English: (It's obvious that Melissa Foster did it.)

Japanese: （トランクに死体をかくして、 《被害者》になりすまし‥‥）
English: (She hid the body in the trunk and disguised herself as the victim...)

Japanese: （ニセの《目撃写真》を デッチ上げた‥‥）
English: (She set up the camera to snap a fake photo of them together...)

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、あのコネコちゃんは そんなコトをした‥‥？
English: The only question is... why did she do it...?

Japanese: 決まってます。
English: Well, isn't that obvious?

Japanese: 彼女こそが‥‥《真犯人》だから！
English: She's the true criminal!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
とにかく、無久井　里子さんの 意識が戻るのを待ちましょう。
English: Well, we'll have to wait for Ms. Foster to compose herself before we start again.

Japanese: それまで、審理は中断します。
English: Until then, this court is in recess.

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側とも、控え室で 待機しておくように。
English: The defense and the prosecution are instructed to wait in the lobby.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥了解した。
English: Understood.

Japanese: それでは！ 審理を中断します！
English: Very well! This court is in recess!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
